summary: Built cross-dimension aggregation. Created aggregate.py (Parts A+B+C) and extended render-report.py with --overall mode (Part D). Fixed a slug mismatch: parity dimension file is parity-latest.json, not cross_platform_parity-latest.json. aggregate.py carries cohort through from per-dimension results. First run: avg_overall=3.67, dist 3×9/4×1.

3_worst_analysis.file.mdx_3.2: Parity=2 is the anchor dragging it down (20% weight × 2 = 0.40 vs 4 × 0.20 = 0.80). Flutter coverage is sparse. Combined with all other dims at 3-4, parity is the highest-ROI fix.
3_worst_analysis.image-handling_3.4: Uniform 3s except Examples=4. No single catastrophic dimension — this page needs broad uplift: add parameter tables (Completeness), reduce interleaved snippets (AI-consumability), add Flutter parity.
3_worst_analysis.send-a-message_3.5: Parity=2 again (Flutter=0 blocks) despite being the most complex page. Examples=5 is strong. The parity score (0.40 weighted contribution lost) is the single biggest lever.

notes.surprises: message-flagging scores 3.85 despite Clarity=2 — its high accuracy (5) and strong examples (5) offset the clarity penalty. The 25% accuracy weight keeps drift-clean pages afloat even when prose quality is weak.
notes.accuracy_note: 1 of 10 pages scored accuracy=5 (message-flagging, 0 iOS warnings). Other 9 pages score accuracy=4 due to iOS doc-as-test warnings — not actionable (Swift preamble-style warnings, not real doc errors). Accuracy is effectively a ceiling-depressor for all pages at this moment, not a differentiator.
notes.fix_note_for_prepare_py: LLM_DIMENSIONS used 'cross_platform_parity' as slug but the file is named 'parity-latest.json'. Fixed in aggregate.py. Also noted: prepare.py was patched in task 0071 to support {llms_txt} substitution.
